title: Fix release workflow strict Smoke dispatch ref
outOfScope: Release version or release metadata changes
Changing Smoke acceptance or source SHA matching
Publishing a release before the fixed workflow is verified

sources.reason: Failing strict Smoke dispatch uses an invalid bare SHA ref.

problemStatement: The release workflow passes the verified commit SHA as a workflow_dispatch ref, but GitHub rejects a bare SHA for workflow dispatch with HTTP 422 before strict Smoke can run.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Strict Smoke dispatch uses a valid branch/ref while still filtering the run by exact SOURCE_COMMIT.
The workflow still requires exact source SHA identity and watches the dispatched Smoke run to completion.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix changes only the workflow dispatch ref; exact source matching and Smoke result gates remain unchanged.

executionDecision.reason: The failure is deterministic and the minimal correction is identified.

preReviewWarnings: Do not weaken SOURCE_COMMIT matching or Smoke result checks.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record the failure evidence and regression verification.

acceptance: The release workflow dispatches Smoke with a valid ref derived from the release workflow ref.
The dispatched Smoke run is still selected by exact SOURCE_COMMIT and event, then watched with exit status.
Workflow tests and full quality pass.
guidelines: Make the smallest workflow change supported by the observed GitHub error.

intent.problem: GitHub rejects the strict Smoke dispatch ref before the release gate can run.
intent.rationale: A branch ref is valid for workflow dispatch, while the existing commit filter preserves immutable source identity.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized the complete release cycle.

rollbackNote: Revert the one workflow ref expression and its regression assertion if the release gate remains invalid.
